subject: Chemistry
title: Alchemy's Legacy in Modern Science
description: One-minute skill: Alchemy's Legacy in Modern Science.

hook.text: Alchemy gave us lab equipment, chemical techniques, new elements, and the very idea that matter can be transformed.

buildup.text: Distillation, crystallization, calcination, and sublimation—all techniques invented by alchemists—are used daily in labs.

discovery.text: Alchemists discovered phosphorus, zinc, arsenic, antimony, and bismuth—five elements found through blind experimentation.

twist.text: Carl Jung saw alchemy as psychology—the 'Great Work' was really about transforming the self, not lead into gold.

climax.text: From Egyptian metallurgy to nuclear transmutation, the alchemical dream of transformation became science's central project.

punchline.text: Wrong theory, right instinct. Alchemy's children run the modern world.

quiz.question: What lasting contributions did alchemy make to modern science?
options: Lab techniques, discovered elements, and the concept that matter can be transformed
The periodic table and atomic theory
The laws of thermodynamics and conservation of energy
